message:
refactor(storage): plumb allow_uncorrelated_in through label-filter helper

The previous commit gated the uncorrelated-IN vs correlated-EXISTS
choice on `params.is_empty()` — captured at the top of
`append_label_membership_filters` itself. That works for the case
where the helper is the FIRST predicate-appender after the FROM
clause, but it's the wrong observable: the call site knows what
predicates ITS code is appending and whether they bind params; the
helper has no way to know which appends will follow.

Concretely, three call sites in `sqlite.rs` (`list_issues`,
`count_issues_with_filters`, the search filter path) ask the same
question — "is this label predicate the only narrowing predicate I
have, or has my caller already narrowed by status / priority /
timestamp / parent?" — and the answer is fundamentally a property
of the call site's plan, not of the helper's local view of the
params Vec at entry.

Replace the in-helper `params.is_empty()` capture with an explicit
`allow_uncorrelated_in: bool` parameter. Each call site computes
its OWN `label_filters_can_use_uncorrelated_in` boolean from the
specific filter fields it cares about — currently shaped roughly
as:

    let label_filters_can_use_uncorrelated_in =
        filters.statuses.is_none()
        && filters.priorities.is_none()
        && filters.assignee.is_none()
        && filters.creator.is_none()
        && filters.since.is_none()
        && filters.until.is_none()
        && filters.parent.is_none()  // count path only
        && filters.created_after.is_none()
        && filters.updated_after.is_none();

This is strictly more conservative than the old behavior (an
empty `params` vec was a sufficient but not necessary condition
for the IN form to be the right plan), but it's the right
shape for adding more nuanced predicates: a future caller that
appends a single cheap predicate before the label filter doesn't
have to play games with param-vec ordering to get the IN plan back
— it just sets `allow_uncorrelated_in = true` after computing
its own predicate inventory.

The body of the helper is unchanged below the dedupe step. Three
call sites land their per-site boolean computation alongside the
existing predicate setup, then thread it through the helper call.
The boolean is named identically across call sites
(`label_filters_can_use_uncorrelated_in`) so future
callers can grep for the convention and do the same.

No functional regression — every existing call site computes a
boolean that's at least as restrictive as `params.is_empty()`
was at the same point in the function — but the indirection makes
the predicate inventory explicit at the site that owns it.
